pi_items.source: BioSemi Data Format standard file extension confirmed by https://www.biosemi.com/faq/file_format.htm and OpenBCI documentation at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/
Task description explicitly requires 'record for at least 5 seconds' in the task steps

BDF file size depends on number of channels and sampling rate. For typical OpenBCI with 8 EEG + 1 status channel at 250 Hz, 5 seconds yields ~36KB. Minimal configs (2-4 channels) could be 8-20KB. The 10KB threshold is heuristic and configuration-dependent.

BioSemi documentation confirms BDF file header starts with byte 0xFF (255 decimal). See: https://www.biosemi.com/faq/file_format.htm and https://www.teuniz.net/edfbrowser/bdfplus%20format%20description.html
BioSemi documentation confirms bytes 2-8 of BDF header contain 'BIOSEMI' in ASCII. The complete signature is 0xFF followed by 'BIOSEMI' (7 bytes). See: https://www.biosemi.com/faq/file_format.htm

OpenBCI documentation states default recordings location is 'User/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings' on all operating systems. For user 'ga' on Linux, this is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings'. Confirmed by install_openbci.sh which creates this directory structure. See: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/

privileged_info_summary: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023) supports BDF+ recording format with SYNTHETIC data source. BDF files use .bdf extension with a header signature of 0xFF followed by 'BIOSEMI' in the first 8 bytes. OpenBCI GUI records to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings by default. The task requires minimum 5 seconds of recording. BDF file size depends on channel count and sampling rate (typically 8 EEG + 1 status channel at 250 Hz, resulting in ~36KB for 5 seconds).
